_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v2 (2026-05-17): Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=54. Pipeline figure corrected from '3,000 graduates/year' to '~6,600 E&E-related bachelor's-degree graduates/year' per government data. Singapore EP minimum corrected from SGD5,000 to SGD5,600 (non-finance) and SGD6,200 (finance). 'GLCs and MNCs absorb easily / SMEs cannot' (UNVERIFIED) softened to DOSM MSME-share framing. 'Multinationals choosing between Penang and Batam' (speculative) dropped. 'Pipeline, brain drain, enforcement — policy moves none' (analytical) reframed as separate-lever observation.

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — 'Pipeline: 3,000 graduates/year' → '~6,600 E&E-related bachelor's-degree graduates per year' (government data).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — Singapore EP minimum 'SGD5,000 (~RM16,600)' → 'SGD5,600 (~RM18,500) for most sectors, SGD6,200 (~RM20,500) for financial services'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'GLCs and MNCs absorb easily / SMEs cannot' replaced with DOSM MSME share (39.5% of GDP, 48.7% employment).
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Multinationals choosing between Penang and Batam now see Malaysia as more expensive' dropped (speculative without comparative cost data).
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Thailand and Vietnam offer alternatives with lower barriers' dropped (too broad).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Succession planning mandatory on paper but rarely enforced' → 'The official FAQ says monitoring will use documentation and periodic reports' (policy hasn't taken effect yet).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'RM10,000 was too low and undercut locals' replaced with official 'focus expatriate hiring on high-impact expertise' framing.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'This policy moves none of pipeline quality, brain drain, and enforcement' softened to 'separate set of policies' (policy does have replacement-plan mechanism).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Protection without pipeline investment produces paper compliance' → 'The risk is that... becomes paper compliance' (framed as risk, not established outcome).

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): 1 June 2026 effective date; RM10K → RM20K Category I; Category III 5-year cap with mandatory replacement plans; local-talent-priority framing; 60,000 semiconductor target by 2030.
